title: Step 1: Confirm a clean FlowPlane workspace
detail: The recording begins with no mappings or active runtime work. Authentication and the tenant context remain available for the live workflow.

title: Understand the end-to-end runtime path
detail: One raw Kafka stream will be processed independently by a Flink job and a Kafka Connect sink, with no scripted downstream inserts.

title: Preserve real tenant and team ownership
detail: Existing team records remain visible because the demo resets runtime work, not the authentication or ownership model used by governance.

title: Verify the runtime monitor starts empty
detail: No demo connector or Flink job is registered yet, establishing the before-state for later runtime cards and physical instances.

title: Verify Kafka Connect starts with zero connectors
detail: Control Center shows no connector before FlowPlane registration, proving the connector that appears later is created during this recording.

title: Step 2: Register Kafka Connect through FlowPlane
detail: The live FlowPlane form defines one logical Mongo sink runtime and its expected two-task execution model.

title: Keep Control Center visible before execution
detail: The Connect screen still shows zero connectors immediately before the UI-issued command is executed against the prepared worker.

title: Run the exact connector command supplied by the UI
detail: The disclosed PowerShell command applies the runtime-specific configuration while Control Center remains visible for independent confirmation.

title: Confirm the connector appears with two running tasks
detail: Control Center now shows the newly created Mongo sink connector and confirms that both configured tasks are running.

title: Inspect the two physical Connect task executions
detail: The connector detail view exposes task 0 and task 1, matching the two partitions of the raw Kafka topic.

title: Step 3: Prepare the Flink execution runtime
detail: Runtime Monitor remains visible before submission, preserving the clean before-state for the Flink job and its physical execution instance.

title: Run the real Flink registration command
detail: PowerShell visibly executes 03-register-flink-job.ps1, submitting the live Flink job with its FlowPlane wrapper and renewable short-lived authentication.

title: Confirm both logical runtimes are online and idle
detail: Fresh heartbeats prove the connector and Flink job are authenticated, healthy, and ready before any mapping artifact is assigned.

title: Understand runtime type, logical runtime, and instance
detail: The type describes the integration, each logical runtime represents a managed connector or job, and physical instances represent the workers or tasks doing the work.

title: Inspect the Flink physical execution instance
detail: The expanded Flink runtime shows its job execution identity, online state, last-seen timestamp, and available telemetry fields.

title: Step 4: Create and govern mapping version 1.0.0
detail: The mapping follows the normal review, approval, publication, and deployment lifecycle rather than bypassing governance state.

title: Compile and publish an immutable runtime artifact
detail: FlowPlane produces a versioned artifact whose identifier, version, and hash can be correlated with every downstream runtime result.

title: Run the saved valid-record simulation
detail: Simulation Studio previews the transformed payload through backend FlowPlane processing before the mapping is assigned to live runtimes.

title: Run the controlled invalid-record simulation
detail: The same mapping rejects the missing event ID and exposes the field error that runtime policy will later route to each DLQ.

title: Inspect Kafka topics before live traffic
detail: Control Center provides the before-state for the raw, transformed, Flink DLQ, and Connect DLQ topics.

title: Confirm transformed and DLQ topics are empty
detail: The downstream Kafka topics contain no demo records before deployment, so all later messages can be attributed to runtime processing.

title: Assign version 1.0.0 to Connect and Flink
detail: The same governed artifact is targeted to both logical runtimes so their outcomes can be compared from one source contract.

title: Run the version-1 raw producer in PowerShell
detail: The visible 05-produce-and-verify-v1.ps1 command writes one valid and one invalid event only to flowplane.demo.orders.raw. No script writes downstream or Mongo data.

title: Wait for both runtime wrappers to acknowledge processing
detail: FlowPlane waits for real runtime completion before checking outputs; the workflow never fabricates transformed, DLQ, or sink results.

title: Return to Control Center for the after-state
detail: The topic list is revisited only after Flink and Connect finish processing the two raw source events.

title: Inspect the two raw version-1 source events
detail: Control Center shows the valid and invalid inputs with their distinct Kafka partitions and offsets.

title: Inspect the Flink transformed output
detail: The live Flink job creates one transformed Kafka record for the valid event, preserving run, version, and artifact provenance.

title: Inspect the Flink DLQ output
detail: The invalid event creates one structured Flink DLQ envelope with the field error and exact raw Kafka source coordinate.

title: Inspect the Kafka Connect DLQ output
detail: Kafka Connect independently creates one DLQ envelope for the invalid event while the sink connector remains healthy.

title: Inspect the Kafka Connect Mongo sink document
detail: Mongo Express reads the document written by the real sink connector for the valid event. The demo never inserts it manually.

title: Open live physical-instance telemetry
detail: Runtime cards expose the Flink instance, Connect worker, and Connect tasks with online state, heartbeats, counters, heap, and GC signals.

title: Diagnose and contain the invalid event
detail: FlowPlane links each field-level failure to its runtime, artifact version, validation rule, and raw Kafka coordinate without stopping processing.

title: Complete the version-1 runtime proof
detail: Version 1 passes only after each runtime produces exactly one valid downstream outcome and one invalid DLQ outcome.

title: Step 5: Publish mapping version 1.1.0 with three additions
detail: v2 adds customerRiskBand, mappingSchemaVersion, and runtimeMetadataField as a second immutable artifact while preserving version-1 history.

title: Simulate the version-2 candidate before promotion
detail: Valid and invalid samples exercise the candidate artifact before either runtime is allowed to activate it.

title: Step 6: Queue the Connect candidate replay gate
detail: The connector is instructed to evaluate historical raw Kafka records against version 1.1.0 inside its runtime wrapper.

title: Process historical raw events inside Connect
detail: Candidate replay uses the connector runtime and recorded source events; it cannot pass through a manually fabricated downstream record.

title: Confirm the Connect compatibility gate completes
detail: FlowPlane accepts the candidate only after the connector reports a completed replay result for the historical inputs.

title: Step 7: Review the downstream schema difference
detail: The candidate output is compared with the registered downstream topic subject before the Flink schema gate is executed.

title: Run the schema check on the live Flink job
detail: The Flink runtime validates version 1.1.0 against Schema Registry and must report a passed result before deployment.

title: Step 8: Deploy version 1.1.0 after both gates pass
detail: The candidate can target Connect and Flink only after replay completion and downstream schema compatibility are confirmed.

title: Activate the approved candidate on both runtimes
detail: The deployment view tracks version 1.1.0 as it is assigned to the same connector and Flink job used for version 1.0.0.

title: Run the version-2 raw producer in PowerShell
detail: The visible 08-produce-and-verify-v2.ps1 command writes one valid and one invalid v2 event only to the raw topic, then waits for runtime-owned outputs.

title: Inspect the complete raw topic history
detail: Control Center now shows four raw events: one valid and one invalid input for each immutable mapping version.

title: Verify the version-2 Flink transformation
detail: The live Flink job creates exactly one valid v2 output containing customerRiskBand, mappingSchemaVersion v1.1.0, and runtimeMetadataField v2-runtime-visible.

title: Verify the version-2 Flink DLQ result
detail: The invalid input creates one additional Flink DLQ envelope with mapping, artifact, error, and raw Kafka provenance.

title: Verify the version-2 Connect DLQ result
detail: Kafka Connect creates its own additional DLQ envelope while the connector continues processing the valid sink record.

title: Compare the two governed runtime outcomes
detail: The same valid-and-invalid input pattern reaches distinct runtime-owned outputs under two immutable artifact versions.

title: Verify the second Mongo sink document
detail: Mongo Express shows the new v2 document beside the preserved v1 result. Kafka Connect inserted both valid records with their version and artifact provenance.

title: Confirm runtime instances remain online
detail: The Flink execution, Connect worker, and both Connect tasks continue reporting fresh last-seen values after version-2 processing.

title: Compare version 1.0.0 with version 1.1.0
detail: The version comparison ties both successful and invalid outcomes to the correct immutable artifact and source coordinates.

title: Confirm version 1.1.0 is active
detail: The mapping view shows the latest artifact deployed to both runtimes while the superseded version remains preserved for history.

title: Confirm authentication and heartbeat continuity
detail: Both wrappers renew short-lived credentials and maintain fresh telemetry throughout the complete recording.

title: Review the final runtime deployment state
detail: Both logical targets show the governed candidate assignment after processing, replay, and schema verification complete.

title: Generate the evidence-bound verdict
detail: The report is generated only after topic counts, Mongo documents, DLQ envelopes, runtime gates, and producer boundaries are verified.

title: Review the operational audit trail
detail: Creation, approval, publication, deployment, replay, schema validation, and runtime targets remain attributable to actors and timestamps.

title: Step 9: Inspect the final mapping lifecycle
detail: The lifecycle view connects one mapping, two immutable artifacts, one connector, and one Flink job to the recorded decisions.

title: Close with the runtime-only evidence verdict
detail: The final PASS confirms scripts wrote raw input only and every transformed, DLQ, and Mongo result came from real runtime processing.
